pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates the DOCX file at this exact path using python-docx.
task.json description and export_result.sh explicitly specify and check this path.
task.json description instructs these headers, and verifier.py verifies their presence in the table.
task.json requires this exact document header, and verifier.py checks for its presence.
The synthetic data in setup_task.sh has 4 pipe-delimited fields per line, and verifier.py requires len(table.columns) >= 4.

pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly states this in comments and uses recognizable lines ('They\'re coming to get you, Barbra!') from the screenplay.

privileged_info_summary: The task dynamically generates a synthetic DOCX file at /home/ga/Documents/raw_dialogue.docx using python-docx inside setup_task.sh, containing an excerpt from the Night of the Living Dead screenplay. The output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/NOTLD_VO_Script.docx and contain exactly 4 table columns with the headers 'Audio ID', 'Character', 'Voice Direction', and 'Dialogue', as well as the document header 'Project: NOTLD - Studio VO Script', all of which are strictly enforced by the task instructions and export/verifier scripts.
